title: Understanding Centripetal Force
text: Centripetal force is the force that keeps an object moving in a circular path. It is directed towards the center of the circle.

title: Formula for Centripetal Force
text: The formula for centripetal force \(F_c\) is given by \(F_c=m*v**2/r\), where \(m\) is the mass, \(v\) is the velocity, and \(r\) is the radius of the circle.

title: Converting Revolutions per Minute to Velocity
text: To find the velocity \(v\), convert the angular speed from revolutions per minute to meters per second. Use the formula \(v=2*\pi*r*(\text{revolutions per second})\).

title: Calculate the Velocity
text: Given the angular speed is $$3.00$$ $$\frac{rev}{min}$$, convert it to meters per second using the radius $$8.00$$ $$m$$.

title: Calculate the Centripetal Force
text: Now that you have the velocity, use the formula \(F_c=m*v**2/r\) to find the centripetal force.
